lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY guarded lifecycle for the eventstreams (Kafka DBaaS) WRITE endpoints. Does NOT provision a billable cluster: soft-captures an EXISTING cluster_id from the read-only list; when none exists the {cluster_id}/{instance_group_id}/{block_storage_group_id} tokens stay literal and normalize to the catalog '*' key so each endpoint is still CALLED and RECORDED. Every mutating step optional + grouped + broad expect_status; heavy:true gates behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY. | BODY FIX 2026-06-11 (lever 4, docs-derived pending live validation, all from data/api_docs.json models): add-instances now sends EventStreamsClusterAddInstancesRequest {instance_count, service_ip_addresses} (the old {instances:[{role_type,...}]} array was a DIFFERENT model and could never validate); set-maintenance now sends MaintenanceRequest {start_day_of_week,start_minute,start_time,term_hour} (the old {period_hour,starting_day_of_week,starting_time} was the create-time MaintenanceOption shape, wrong model for POST .../maintenance) with the same values the mysql/pg heavy lifecycles use for the identical model; set-security-group-rules now sends UpdateSecurityGroupRulesRequest {add_ip_addresses,del_ip_addresses} exactly as the doc request example (both empty = no-op, reversible by definition; the old security_group_rules rule-object array was a different service's shape); set-parameter-values now sends ParametersRequest entries {id,new_value,old_value} with id/value soft-captured from GET .../parameters and new_value==old_value==applied_value (documented no-op, reversible). ADDED no-body sub-ops from api_docs (body 'None'): sync-state, parameters/sync, unset-maintenance (DELETE, reversible — set-maintenance precedes it). patch-minor-version DELIBERATELY keeps the empty body (MinorPatchDbEngineRequest is a version upgrade — not obviously reversible, excluded by the conservative sub-op rule; the 4xx still records C2). start/stop NOT added: stopping an adopted live cluster is not obviously reversible operationally. eventstreamscreatecluster stays OUT of heavy-shared-dbaas (undocumented topology value_error) — the docs-derived topology facts for a future attempt are recorded in knowledge/formal/services/data-analytics__eventstreams.yaml.

steps.name: list-parameter-values
steps._note: eventstreamslistparametervalues (read-only) — feeds the no-op set-parameter-values below; contents[].id/applied_value fields from the api_docs response example.

steps.name: add-instances
steps._note: EventStreamsClusterAddInstancesRequest per api_docs: instance_count (int, required) + service_ip_addresses (array[string], required; doc request example sends [""] — empty string = platform-assigned, same convention as the validated mysql create service_ip_address:""). public_ip_ids optional, omitted. Docs-derived, pending live validation.

steps.name: set-parameter-values
steps._note: ParametersRequest -> ParameterRequest {id,new_value,old_value} (all required per api_docs model). new_value==old_value==applied_value captured from the live cluster = documented no-op write, reversible. With no live cluster the tokens stay literal and the call records C2. Docs-derived.

steps.name: set-security-group-rules
steps._note: UpdateSecurityGroupRulesRequest per api_docs: add_ip_addresses/del_ip_addresses (both optional arrays); doc request example sends both empty = no-op, reversible. Docs-derived.

steps.name: set-maintenance
steps._note: MaintenanceRequest per api_docs (start_day_of_week enum MON..SUN, start_minute, start_time, term_hour pattern ^(0\.5|[1-5]\.5|[1-9]|1[0-9]|2[0-4])$) — same model and values as the mysql/pg heavy set-maintenance steps. Reversed by unset-maintenance below. Docs-derived.

steps.name: unset-maintenance
steps._note: eventstreamsunsetmaintenance (no body per api_docs) — reverses set-maintenance; config-only delete, no resource destroyed. Docs-derived.

steps.name: sync-parameters
steps._note: eventstreamssyncparametervalues (no body per api_docs) — read-side reconciliation, safe. Docs-derived.

steps.name: sync-cluster-state
steps._note: eventstreamssyncclusterstate (no body per api_docs) — read-side reconciliation, safe. Docs-derived.

steps.name: patch-minor-version
steps._note: Body kept DELIBERATELY empty: MinorPatchDbEngineRequest {dbaas_engine, software_version} is a version upgrade — not obviously reversible, excluded by the conservative sub-op rule. The guaranteed 4xx records C2 only.

steps.name: set-block-storage-size
steps._note: ResizeBlockStorageGroupRequest size_gb min 16 per api_docs; 20 stays a coverage-only call (no live cluster expected).

lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY read lifecycle for eventstreams' two id-bound GETs (READ-ONLY, never mutates). Lists /v1/engine-versions and soft-captures the id under the EXACT catalog path-param name (dbaas_engine_version_id) so the final probe-reads step exercises eventstreamslistengineversionproperties. KNOWN-HARD: eventstreamsshowrequest (/v1/requests/{request_id}) — request_id is only returned by async write ops and is NOT exposed by any list/collection GET. FIXED 2026-06-11: the step previously used a LITERAL placeholder uuid, which records ZERO static coverage (the Wave-1 catalog-matching gotcha: literal id segments never normalize to '*'); it now uses the {request_id} token backed by a deliberately-never-matching capture_soft ($.request_id does not exist in the engine-versions list response), so the token stays literal at runtime (guaranteed 404 = C2 recorded) AND the step matches the catalog key statically — closing the /v1/requests/{request_id} GAP-get-id shared path. Every step optional + grouped + broad expect_status.

steps.name: list-engine-versions
steps._note: request_id capture_soft is DELIBERATELY a path that never matches this response — see lifecycle _note.
